The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 441a9c92). ## Issue to Resolve Bug on parameter's class that reached maximum line ## Expected Behavior No error. ## Observed Behavior Error. ## Steps to Reproduce I have parameter lambda code like this, let say my parameter is reached maximum limit of line : There is error detected on `open bracket (`. I already clicked `Format with ktlint`, but still error. ## Your Environment * Version of ktlint used: 0.43.2 * Version of Gradle used (if applicable): AGP 7 * Operating System and version: MacOS Big Sur ## Additional Context from Issue Discussion Next time, please post your code instead of an image. It is more convenient when trying to reproduce a problem. The code below is comparable to your example: ``` // Assume, the line breaks at the pipe symbol on the right -> | val foo1: ((s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String) -> Unit)? = null ``` The `ktlint --verbose --relative` command results in: ``` src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:1: Exceeded max line length (80) (cannot be auto-corrected) (max-line-length) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:13: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:25: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:37: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:49: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:61: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:73: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:83: Missing newline before ")" (parameter-list-wrapping) ``` From this it can be seen that both the `max-line-length` and the `parameter-list-wrapping` report violations in the original code. Auto-correct by the `parameter-list-wrapping` resolves the max line length violation and results in code below: ``` // Assume, next line exceeds the max line length -> | val foo1: ( ( s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String ) -> Unit )? = null ``` but that same rule immediately reports now another lint error as you reported as well: ``` src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:3:5: Unnecessary newline before "(" (parameter-list-wrapping) ``` So far, I have found no way to refactor the code manually in such a way that the code is accepted by both *ktlint* as well as by the IntelliJ default code formatting which nowadays is a requirement when we make (bugfixes on) rules. For example, the previous code sample will be transformed by IntelliJ default formatting to following: ``` // Assume, next line exceeds the max line length -> | val foo1: ( ( s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String ) -> Unit )? = null ``` This is horrible code. For now, I see no other way for you to disable both rules and stick with one really long line: ``` val foo1: ((s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String) -> Unit)? = null // ktlint-disable max-line-length parameter-list-wrapping ``` Maybe, someone else has a briljant idea how to the resulting code should look like in such a way that both ktlint as well as IntelliJ default formatting do not alter that code. Next step would then be to fix the rules do that the code will be formatted as such. ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `CHANGELOG.md` - `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ParameterListWrappingRule.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

1â†’# Changelog
  2â†’All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3â†’This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4â†’
  5â†’## Unreleased
  6â†’
  7â†’### Custom Rule Providers need to prepare for Kotlin 1.9
  8â†’
  9â†’In Kotlin 1.9 the extension points of the embedded kotlin compiler will change. Ktlint only uses the `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` extension point. This extension is not yet supported in 1.9, neither is it documented ([#KT-58704](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-58704/Support-and-document-extension-point-org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler)). Without this extension point it might happen that your custom rules will throw exceptions during runtime. See [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981).
 10â†’
 11â†’In Ktlint, 7 out of 77 rules needed small and sometimes bigger changes to become independent of the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler`. The impact on your custom rules may vary dependent on the way the autocorrect has been implemented. When manipulating `ASTNode`s there seems to be no impact. When, manipulating `PsiElement`s, some functions consistently result in a runtime exception.
 12â†’
 13â†’Based on the refactoring of the rules as provided by `ktlint-ruleset-standard` in Ktlint `0.49.x` the suggested refactoring is as follows:
 14â†’
 15â†’* Replace `LeafElement.replaceWithText(String)` with `LeafElement.rawReplaceWithText(String)`.
 16â†’* Replace `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)` with `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)`. Note that this method inserts the new node (first) argument *before* the second argument node and as of that is not a simple replacement of the `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)`.
 17â†’* Replace `PsiElement.replace(PsiElement)` with a sequence of `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)` and `AstNode.removeChild(AstNode)`.
 18â†’
 19â†’Be aware that your custom rules might use other functions which also throw exceptions when the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is no longer supported.
 20â†’
 21â†’In order to help you to analyse and fix the problems with your custom rules, ktlint temporarily supports to disable the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` using a flag. This flag is available in the Ktlint CLI and in the `KtlintRuleEngine`. By default, the extension point is enabled like it was in previous versions of ktlint.
 22â†’
 23â†’At least you should analyse the problems by running your test suits by running ktlint and disabling the extension point. Next you can start with fixing and releasing the updated rules. All rules in this version of ktlint have already been refactored and are not dependent on the extension point anymore. In Ktlint CLI the flag is to be activated with parameter `--disable-kotlin-extension-point`. API Consumers that use the `KtlintRuleEngine` directly, have to set property `enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint` to `false`.
 24â†’
 25â†’At this point in time, it is not yet decided what the next steps will be. Ktlint might drop the support of the extension points entirely. Or, if the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is fully supported at the time that ktlint will be based on kotlin 1.9 it might be kept. In either case, the flag will be dropped in a next version of ktlint.
 26â†’
 27â†’### Added
 28â†’
 29â†’* Add new experimental rule `binary-expression-wrapping`. This rule wraps a binary expression in case the max line length is exceeded ([#1940](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1940))
 30â†’* Add flag to disable extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` to analyse impact on custom rules [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981)
 31â†’* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-file` for all code styles. A kotlin (script) file may not be empty ([#1074](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1074))
 32â†’* Add new experimental rule `statement-wrapping` which ensures function, class, or other blocks statement body doesn't start or end at starting or ending braces of the block ([#1938](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1938))
 33â†’* Add new experimental rule `blank-line-before-declaration`. This rule requires a blank line before class, function or property declarations ([#1939](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1939))
 34â†’
 35â†’### Removed
 36â†’
 37â†’### Fixed
 38â†’
 39â†’* Do not flag a (potential) mutable extension property in case the getter is annotated or prefixed with a modifier `property-naming` ([#2024](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2024))
 40â†’* Do not merge an annotated expression body with the function signature even if it fits on a single line ([#2043](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2043))
 41â†’* Ignore property with name `serialVersionUID` in `property-naming` ([#2045](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2045))
 42â†’
 43â†’### Changed
 44â†’
 45â†’* Fix Java interoperability issues with `RuleId` and `RuleSetId` classes. Those classes were defined as value classes in `0.49.0` and `0.49.1`. Although the classes were marked with `@JvmInline` it seems that it is not possible to uses those classes from Java base API Consumers like Spotless. The classes have now been replaced with data classes ([#2041](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2041))
 46â†’* Update dependency `info.picocli:picocli` to v4.7.3
 47â†’* Update dependency `org.junit.jupiter:junit-jupiter` to v5.9.3
 48â†’* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.21` and Kotlin version to `1.8.21`.
 49â†’* Update dependency io.github.detekt.sarif4k:sarif4k to v0.4.0

 52â†’
 53â†’### Added
 54â†’
 55â†’### Removed
 56â†’
 57â†’### Fixed
 58â†’* Store path of file containing a lint violation relative to the location of the baseline file itself ([#1962](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1962))
 59â†’* Print absolute path of file in lint violations when flag "--relative" is not specified in Ktlint CLI ([#1963](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1963)) 
 60â†’* Handle parameter `--code-style=android_studio` in Ktlint CLI identical to deprecated parameter `--android` ([#1982](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1982))

 62â†’* Allow to 'unset' the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than` when using `ktlint_official` code style `function-signature` ([#1977](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1977))

The `no-single-line-block-comment` rule is added as experimental rule to the `ktlint_official` code style, but it can be enabled explicitly for the other code styles as well. ([#1980](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1980))
 67â†’* Clean-up unwanted logging dependencies ([#1998](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1998))
 68â†’* Fix directory traversal for patterns referring to paths outside of current working directory or any of it child directories ([#2002](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2002))
 69â†’
 70â†’### Changed
 71â†’
 72â†’* Moved class `Baseline` from `ktlint-cli` to `ktlint-cli-reporter-baseline` so that Baseline functionality is reusable for API Consumers.

 74â†’## [0.49.0] - 2023-04-21
 75â†’
 76â†’WARNING: This version of KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgraded before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
 77â†’
 78â†’All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 79â†’* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
 80â†’* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
 81â†’* The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 82â†’* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 83â†’* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 84â†’
 85â†’### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 86â†’
 87â†’The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 88â†’
 89â†’Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 90â†’* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
 91â†’* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
 92â†’* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 93â†’* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 94â†’
 95â†’### Promote experimental rules to non-experimental
 96â†’
 97â†’The rules below have been promoted to non-experimental rules:
 98â†’* [block-comment-initial-star-alignment](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#block-comment-initial-star-alignment)
 99â†’* [class-naming](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#classobject-naming)
100â†’* [comment-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#comment-wrapping)
101â†’* [function-return-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-return-type-spacing)
102â†’* [function-start-of-body-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-start-of-body-spacing)
103â†’* [function-type-reference-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-type-reference-spacing)
104â†’* [fun-keyword-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#fun-keyword-spacing)
105â†’* [kdoc-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#kdoc-wrapping)
106â†’* [modifier-list-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#modifier-list-spacing)
107â†’* [nullable-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#nullable-type-spacing)
108â†’* [spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis)
109â†’* [unnecessary-parentheses-before-trailing-lambda](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#unnecessary-parenthesis-before-trailing-lambda)
110â†’
111â†’Note that this only affects users that have enabled the standard ruleset while having the experimental rules disabled.
112â†’
113â†’### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
114â†’
115â†’This release is intended to be the last release before the 1.0.x release of ktlint. If all goes as planned, the 1.0.x release does not contain any new breaking changes with except of removal of functionality which is deprecated in this release.
116â†’
117â†’This release contains a lot of breaking changes which aims to improve the future maintainability of Ktlint. If you get stuck while migrating, please reach out to us by creating an issue.
118â†’
119â†’#### Experimental rules
120â†’
121â†’Rules in custom rule sets can now be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint if `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or if the rule itself has been enabled explicitly.
122â†’
123â†’When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set as that would require a distinct rule set id. When moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set this would mean that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
124â†’
125â†’#### EditorConfig
126â†’
127â†’Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. New fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
128â†’
129â†’The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
130â†’
131â†’#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
132â†’
133â†’A new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
134â†’
135â†’This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide. But do note that this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
136â†’
137â†’The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
138â†’
139â†’The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Note that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
140â†’
141â†’Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
142â†’
143â†’#### Package restructuring and class relocation
144â†’
145â†’The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
146â†’
147â†’This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
148â†’
149â†’Classes below have been moved from module `ktlint-core` to the new module `ktlint-rule-engine-core`:
150â†’
151â†’| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine-core`                               |
152â†’|----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
153â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig               | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig                            |
154â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties     | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig                            |
155â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.OptInFeatures              | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.OptInFeatures                           |
156â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType                | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType                             |
157â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.package                    | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ASTNodeExtension                        |
158â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.IndentConfig                   | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig                            |
159â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule                           | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule                                    |
160â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleProvider                   | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleProvider                            |
161â†’

Let me understand the issue better. Looking at the code and the issue description:

1. The issue is about a nullable function type like `val foo1: ((s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String) -> Unit)? = null`
2. When the line exceeds max line length, `parameter-list-wrapping` tries to wrap it
3. After wrapping, the same rule reports "Unnecessary newline before '('" error

Looking at the code flow:
1. In `wrapNullableType` (lines 83-121), when a nullable type with a function type inside exceeds max line length, it wraps the parameters by adding newlines after LPAR and before RPAR
2. In `wrapParameterInList` (lines 223-272), when a child is LPAR (lines 229-236), it checks if the previous leaf has a newline and if so, it reports "Unnecessary newline before '('" and deletes it

The issue is that when `wrapNullableType` wraps the nullable type parameters (adding newlines), and then later `wrapParameterList` is called on the VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST inside the FUNCTION_TYPE, it sees the newline before the LPAR that was added by `wrapNullableType` and tries to remove it.

Looking at the condition `isFunctionTypeWrappedInNullableType()` (lines 162-165):
```kotlin
private fun ASTNode.isFunctionTypeWrappedInNullableType(): Boolean {
    require(elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
    return treeParent.elementType == FUNCTION_TYPE && treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == NULLABLE_TYPE
}
```

This function checks if the VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST is inside a FUNCTION_TYPE which is inside a NULLABLE_TYPE. When this is true, `needToWrapParameterList()` returns `false`, which means the rule should skip wrapping this parameter list.
 
The specific LPAR handling reveals a nuanced interaction. The code checks for a previous whitespace leaf containing a newline, which triggers an error and potential deletion. However, this mechanism conflicts with the wrapping logic for nullable function types, creating an unexpected behavior where legitimate newlines get flagged as unnecessary.

Examining the AST structure confirms the complexity: nested parentheses in nullable function types create multiple left parenthesis nodes, complicating the whitespace detection strategy. The current implementation might incorrectly process these nested structural elements, potentially removing valid formatting in function type declarations.

When `wrapNullableType` processes the left parenthesis, it systematically adds whitespace after the outer LPAR, which then interferes with subsequent whitespace checks in `wrapParameterInList`. This subtle interaction between different wrapping methods could trigger unintended formatting modifications.

The whitespace detection mechanism specifically looks for previous whitespace containing newline characters, but the newly inserted whitespace from `wrapNullableType` disrupts this expected pattern, potentially causing unexpected formatting behavior in nullable function type declarations.

Examining the abstract syntax tree reveals a nested structure where the inner LPAR of the VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST exists separately from the outer LPAR of the NULLABLE_TYPE, creating a complex scenario for consistent whitespace handling.

The key insight is how `prevLeaf()` traverses the entire tree, not just immediate siblings. This means the whitespace added by `wrapNullableType` becomes the previous leaf for the inner LPAR, disrupting the expected local context checks.

When processing nested type structures like `((s1: String, ...) -> Unit)?`, the whitespace insertion mechanism inadvertently creates an unexpected leaf relationship that breaks the original assumption about local whitespace placement.

Two potential solutions emerge: either modify the whitespace proximity check to respect parent context boundaries, or adjust the leaf traversal logic to prioritize sibling relationships when detecting unnecessary newlines.

The proposed fixes involve either contextual parent type validation or refining the leaf relationship detection mechanism to maintain the intended formatting rules.

## Summary

I've fixed the bug in `ParameterListWrappingRule` where after wrapping a nullable function type like `((params) -> Unit)?`, the rule incorrectly reported "Unnecessary newline before '('" for the inner opening parenthesis.

### Changes Made:

1. **`ParameterListWrappingRule.kt`**: Modified the `wrapParameterInList` function to check if the whitespace before the LPAR is part of nullable type wrapping. If so, we skip emitting the "Unnecessary newline before '('" error.

2. **`ParameterListWrappingRuleTest.kt`**: Added a test case to verify that an already wrapped nullable function type doesn't report the spurious error.

3. **`CHANGELOG.md`**: Added a changelog entry documenting the fix for issue #1324.

### How the Fix Works:

The fix detects nullable type wrapping by checking if the leaf before the whitespace (`prevPrevLeaf`) is an LPAR that belongs to a NULLABLE_TYPE. In the case of nullable type wrapping, the AST structure is:
```
NULLABLE_TYPE -> LPAR -> WHITESPACE -> FUNCTION_TYPE -> VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST -> LPAR
```

The whitespace is between the outer LPAR and FUNCTION_TYPE, so it's intentional wrapping, not a dangling parenthesis that should be flagged.

This fix correctly handles:
- **Dangling parenthesis in class declarations** (`class ClassA\n(...)`) - still detected âś“
- **Dangling parenthesis in function declarations** (`fun foo\n(...)`) - still detected âś“
- **Nullable function type wrapping** (`(\n    (...) -> Unit)?`) - no longer incorrectly flagged âś“

content.text:
## Summary

I have fixed the bug in `ParameterListWrappingRule` where after wrapping a nullable function type like `((params) -> Unit)?`, the rule incorrectly reported "Unnecessary newline before '('" for the inner opening parenthesis.

### Changes Made:

1. **`ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ParameterListWrappingRule.kt`**: 
   - Modified the `wrapParameterInList` function to detect when whitespace before an LPAR is part of nullable type wrapping
   - Added a check: if the leaf before the whitespace is an LPAR that belongs to a NULLABLE_TYPE, we skip emitting the error

2. **`ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ParameterListWrappingRuleTest.kt`**:
   - Added test case `Issue 1324 - Given an already wrapped nullable function type then do not report unnecessary newline before opening parenthesis`

3. **`CHANGELOG.md`**:
   - Added a changelog entry documenting the fix for issue #1324

### How the Fix Works:

When a nullable function type is wrapped, the AST structure is:
```
NULLABLE_TYPE -> LPAR -> WHITESPACE -> FUNCTION_TYPE -> VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST -> LPAR
```

The fix checks if the whitespace before the inner LPAR is part of this nullable type wrapping by looking at the previous leaf before the whitespace (`prevPrevLeaf`). If it's an LPAR belonging to a NULLABLE_TYPE, the whitespace is intentional wrapping and should not be flagged.

This correctly handles:
- **Dangling parenthesis in class/function declarations** (e.g., `fun foo\n(...)`) - still detected âś“
- **Nullable function type wrapping** (e.g., `(\n    (...) -> Unit)?`) - no longer incorrectly flagged âś“

 74â†’## [0.49.0] - 2023-04-21
 75â†’
 76â†’WARNING: This version of KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgraded before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
 77â†’
 78â†’All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 79â†’* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
 80â†’* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
 81â†’* The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 82â†’* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 83â†’* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 84â†’
 85â†’### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 86â†’
 87â†’The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 88â†’
 89â†’Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 90â†’* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
 91â†’* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
 92â†’* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 93â†’* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 94â†’
 95â†’### Promote experimental rules to non-experimental
 96â†’
 97â†’The rules below have been promoted to non-experimental rules:
 98â†’* [block-comment-initial-star-alignment](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#block-comment-initial-star-alignment)
 99â†’* [class-naming](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#classobject-naming)
100â†’* [comment-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#comment-wrapping)
101â†’* [function-return-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-return-type-spacing)
102â†’* [function-start-of-body-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-start-of-body-spacing)
103â†’* [function-type-reference-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-type-reference-spacing)
104â†’* [fun-keyword-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#fun-keyword-spacing)
105â†’* [kdoc-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#kdoc-wrapping)
106â†’* [modifier-list-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#modifier-list-spacing)
107â†’* [nullable-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#nullable-type-spacing)
108â†’* [spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis)
109â†’* [unnecessary-parentheses-before-trailing-lambda](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#unnecessary-parenthesis-before-trailing-lambda)
110â†’
111â†’Note that this only affects users that have enabled the standard ruleset while having the experimental rules disabled.
112â†’
113â†’### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
114â†’
115â†’This release is intended to be the last release before the 1.0.x release of ktlint. If all goes as planned, the 1.0.x release does not contain any new breaking changes with except of removal of functionality which is deprecated in this release.
116â†’
117â†’This release contains a lot of breaking changes which aims to improve the future maintainability of Ktlint. If you get stuck while migrating, please reach out to us by creating an issue.
118â†’
119â†’#### Experimental rules
120â†’
121â†’Rules in custom rule sets can now be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint if `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or if the rule itself has been enabled explicitly.
122â†’
123â†’When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set as that would require a distinct rule set id. When moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set this would mean that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
124â†’
125â†’#### EditorConfig
126â†’
127â†’Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. New fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
128â†’
129â†’The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
130â†’
131â†’#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
132â†’
133â†’A new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
134â†’
135â†’This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide. But do note that this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
136â†’
137â†’The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
138â†’
139â†’The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Note that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
140â†’
141â†’Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
142â†’
143â†’#### Package restructuring and class relocation
144â†’
145â†’The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
146â†’
147â†’This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
